html:
Exclusive to:

Salt FormationGrowing Salt FormationMined Salt Formation

A Salt Formation is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are stationary obstacles located in Brine Shoal set pieces in the ocean. When hit by a Boat, they cause damage to its health. They might also be destroyed themselves if enough damage is done.

Salt Formations have 4 stages. The stump stage cannot be mined, worked on or destroyed. Mining the small stage or the medium stage drops 1 Salt Crystals and 1 Rocks. Mining the full stage drops 1 Salt Crystals with a 50% chance of one extra and 1 Rocks. It takes 10 Pickaxe hits to mine from the full stage. While Salt Crystals float on the water, some of the Rocks may sink into the ocean and cannot be retrieved. Each stage regrows in 7 to 11 days.

Trivia

  • Salt Formations were introduced in the Salty Dog beta update.
  • Their human-like appearance could be a reference to the biblical story of Lot's wife, in which she was turned into a salt statue.

Gallery

  • All stages of Salt Formation 1.
  • All stages of Salt Formation 2.
  • All stages of Salt Formation 3.

Exclusive to:
Salt FormationGrowing Salt FormationMined Salt Formation
“Are those natural formations?”
–Wilson, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Huh. That's... weird.”
–Willow, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Weird statues.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Those figures can't be natural.”
–Wendy, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“TARGET PRACTICE"”
–WX-78, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Intriguing... just how were those figures constructed?”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Nature's full of amazing things, eh?”
–Woodie, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Odd...”
–Maxwell, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Tis the förm öf a sea nymph!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“They're kind of spooky.”
–Webber, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“What beautiful formations!”
–Warly, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Lumpy”
–Wormwood, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Now that's just unsettlin'”
–Winona, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Interesting.”
–Wortox, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Weird rocks, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“Maybe there's a Medusa nearby... a salt Medusa...”
–Walter, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“It's just salt! N-nothing to be bothered over.”
–Wanda, when examining a full Salt Formation.
“I guess it just grows like that.”
–Wilson, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Okay, hear me out... what if I tried burning it?”
–Willow, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Weird statues growing back.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“The cycle begins anew.”
–Wendy, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“IT IS RECONSTRUCTING”
–WX-78, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Oh my... they seem to form that way naturally.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Lookit that, it's growing back!”
–Woodie, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“It seems to be growing back...”
–Maxwell, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Förmed as if by Ödin's hand!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Hey, it's growing back!”
–Webber, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“The sea is capable of amazing things.”
–Warly, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Growing back”
–Wormwood, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Huh, I wonder why they grow like that?”
–Winona, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Oh joyous day. More salt.”
–Wortox, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“It growing!”
–Wurt, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“I'm glad they grow back!”
–Walter, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“Oh goody, it's growing back.”
–Wanda, when examining a medium or small Salt Formation.
“It's mined... it's all mined!”
–Wilson, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Someone got all the salt already.”
–Willow, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Nothing left for Wolfgang to break.”
–Wolfgang, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“It's been utterly destroyed.”
–Wendy, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“IT HAS BEEN TERMINATED”
–WX-78, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“The salt has already been harvested.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Guess that's that.”
–Woodie, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“It's been mined down to nothing.”
–Maxwell, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Naught but a stump!”
–Wigfrid, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Someone already took all the salt.”
–Webber, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Nothing left, I'm afraid.”
–Warly, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“All gone”
–Wormwood, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Ain't nothin left of that one.”
–Winona, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Heehee, hoho, it had to go.”
–Wortox, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Nothing left for taking.”
–Wurt, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“Nothing left to gather.”
–Walter, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
“I feel a bit better now.”
–Wanda, when examining an empty Salt Formation.
A Salt Formation is a naturally spawning object exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are stationary obstacles located in Brine Shoal set pieces in the ocean. When hit by a Boat, they cause damage to its health. They might also be destroyed themselves if enough damage is done.
Salt Formations have 4 stages. The stump stage cannot be mined, worked on or destroyed. Mining the small stage or the medium stage drops 1 Salt Crystals and 1 Rocks. Mining the full stage drops 1 Salt Crystals with a 50% chance of one extra and 1 Rocks. It takes 10 Pickaxe hits to mine from the full stage. While Salt Crystals float on the water, some of the Rocks may sink into the ocean and cannot be retrieved. Each stage regrows in 7 to 11 days.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Salt Formations were introduced in the Salty Dog beta update.
Their human-like appearance could be a reference to the biblical story of Lot's wife, in which she was turned into a salt statue.

A '''Salt Formation''' is a naturally spawning object exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They are stationary obstacles located in [[Ocean#Brine|Brine Shoal]] set pieces in the ocean. When hit by a [[Boat Kit|Boat]], they cause damage to its health. They might also be destroyed themselves if enough damage is done.

Salt Formations have 4 stages. The stump stage cannot be mined, worked on or destroyed. Mining the small stage or the medium stage drops 1 [[Salt Crystals]] and 1 [[Rocks]]. Mining the full stage drops 1 [[Salt Crystals]] with a 50% chance of one extra and 1 [[Rocks]]. It takes 10 [[Pickaxe]] hits to mine from the full stage. While Salt Crystals float on the water, some of the Rocks may sink into the ocean and cannot be retrieved. Each stage regrows in 7 to 11 days.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Salt Formations were introduced in the ''Salty Dog'' beta update.
* Their human-like appearance could be a reference to the biblical story of [[wikipedia:Lot's wife|Lot's wife]], in which she was turned into a salt statue.

{{Clear}}
